project.name: mm-dlp
project.description: High-performance media extractor and downloading engine built in Rust, consumed by Android via UniFFI.

architecture.core: The core engine (`mm-dlp-core`) uses a trait-based routing system (`PlatformExtractor`) wrapped in a `PlatformRegistry` for easy extensibility. All extraction and scraping logic is async and relies on `tokio` and `reqwest`.
architecture.ffi_bridge: The engine is bridged to Kotlin via UniFFI. - `uniffi.toml` specifies the package mapping. - `mm-dlp.udl` declares the interface for `MmDlpEngine`, progress callbacks, and shared structs. - We removed heavy native/C dependencies (`ffmpeg-sys-next`, `boring-sys`) from `mm-dlp-core` because their `build.rs` scripts caused cross-compilation errors (`libavutil.pc` missing). Native bindings must be handled carefully to maintain smooth FFI generation.
architecture.automation: A shell script (`scripts/generate_bindings.sh`) automates building the `cargo ndk` targets and generating Kotlin files with `uniffi-bindgen`.

rules.rule: NO STUBS: Write complete, functional Rust code. No `todo!()` or placeholders.
SECURITY: Validate paths and avoid directory traversal during downloads.
MODERN RUST: Use `std::sync::LazyLock` for static compilation, no `once_cell`.
DEPENDENCIES: Avoid crates that rely heavily on `pkg-config` or host C-libraries unless absolutely necessary, to keep cross-compilation robust.
